blurb: The Financial Conduct Authority (FCA) regulates financial services firms and markets in the UK, including its work on the use of artificial intelligence in financial services.

hiring.note: Official FCA careers page verified but /vacancies route changed/404; needs browser/manual search for current vacancies.

publications.title: FS25/5: AI Live Testing
publications.summary: FCA feedback statement summarising responses to its April 2025 engagement paper proposing an AI Live Testing service to help firms safely deploy AI.

publications.title: AI in financial services: shaping our approach through industry engagement
publications.summary: The FCA says it will not introduce new AI-specific rules for financial services, instead using existing frameworks while gathering evidence through its AI Input Zone, AI Lab, Supercharged Sandbox and AI Live Testing.

publications.title: Rethinking regulation for the age of AI
publications.summary: FCA chief executive Nikhil Rathi argues that agentic AI, tokenisation, frontier-model dependencies and cyber resilience require a more collaborative, system-wide regulatory posture for UK financial services.

news.title: FCA helps firms to test AI safely
news.summary: FCA press release on supporting firms to test AI, following its September 2025 feedback statement on AI Live Testing.

news.title: FCA announces second cohort for AI Live Testing
news.summary: FCA press release announcing the second cohort of participants in its AI Live Testing programme.

notes: Regulator site. FS25/5 date 2025-09-09 from FCA page datePublished meta. Press release dates from page datePublished meta (2025-12-03 and 2026-04-21). followup_harvest 2026-06-28: added first-party June 2026 FCA AI blog/speech after direct fetch and datePublished verification.
